target.name: Aurora Innovation
target.founder: Chris Urmson, Sterling Anderson, Drew Bagnell

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Aurora Innovation operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector as a public company. The valuation universe for direct comparison is constrained: the sector contains five private peers (Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, and ideaForge) and zero public comparables, with no established public trading reference points for the subsector.

The peer-median triangulation method is not applicable to this valuation. The analysis identified only two same-stage peers and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier comparables within the Drones & Autonomous sector—falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable median-based pricing. Attempting an all-sector median would conflate Aurora's specific business model and market position with materially different subsectors, introducing unacceptable distortion. The private peer set, while informative for directional context, is too small and heterogeneous to anchor a quantitative estimate without additional methodology.

This valuation requires alternative methods not present in the current dataset—such as revenue multiples, discounted cash flow analysis, or precedent M&A transactions—to establish a defensible range. The absence of public comps and the thinness of the private peer universe represent the binding constraint. Any valuation estimate at this stage must acknowledge the high uncertainty inherent in pricing a public frontier-tech company without established sector benchmarks or sufficient comparable transaction data.
